fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Spreads money across large, mid, and small companies for balanced growth. Delivered consistent 21% returns over 3 years with a moderate risk level. Helps build long-term wealth for retirement and children's education.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Focuses on well-established large firms and growing mid-sized companies. Strong 27% 3-year return. Adds growth push while keeping risk manageable. Great for achieving the house goal in 5 years and supplementing long-term needs.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Well-diversified between big and mid-sized Indian companies. Reliable 21% 3-year performance. A portion of your current savings goes here to start building equity value immediately.

fund.name: BARODA BNP PARIBAS ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Baroda BNP Paribas Asset Management India Private Limited

allocations.rationale: Saves income tax under Section 80C. Grows money in equities over 3 years. Since you're in the 30% tax group, this fund gives better post-tax returns than debt options. It helps free up cash flow by reducing tax bills.

fund.name: WhiteOak Capital ELSS Tax Saver Fund Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: Another tax-saving fund with strong long-term returns and a different fund manager. Diversifies your ELSS exposure. Equity taxation benefits you due to your high tax bracket.

fund.name: quant Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Automatically adjusts between stocks and bonds based on market levels, protecting gains during falls. Adds stability to your portfolio. Perfect for moderate risk seekers like you.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or Coin by Zerodha.
Step 2: Set up SIPs for the following:
 - ₹32,000/month in Nippon India Multi Cap
 - ₹16,000/month in Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap
 - ₹8,000/month in Baroda BNP Paribas ELSS
 - ₹8,000/month in WhiteOak Capital ELSS
 - ₹8,000/month in quant Dynamic Asset Allocation
Step 3: Transfer ₹1.5 L lumpsum to ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap.
Step 4: Enable auto-debit from your bank account for SIPs.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all SIPs are running smoothly.
After 1 year: Check if each fund has performed in line with its category average.
If job or income changes: Pause and reassess SIP amounts.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check your portfolio mix. If any category (like equities) has grown to more than 5% above your target, book some profits and move money to underweight areas. Use lumpsum bonuses or idle cash for rebalancing.
plan.rationale: At 35, with 20 years to go, you can afford to grow wealth via equities while staying balanced. Your ₹80,000/month surplus and ₹15-lakh corpus are strong foundations. We're using multi cap and large & mid cap funds to grow money reliably for retirement and kids' education. ELSS funds cut your tax and build equity exposure. The dynamic asset allocation fund protects you when markets get shaky. You're in the 30% tax group, so we've chosen equity-taxed funds (like ELSS and arbitrage hybrid) to keep more money in your pocket after taxes.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will fall temporarily. This is normal and usually recovers over 2-3 years.
Inflation may grow faster than expected, reducing the real value of future savings.
Interest rate hikes can hurt bond prices, affecting hybrid fund returns.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: Buy house with proceeds from debt/hybrid funds and some equity gains
Start shifting small amounts to safer options as retirement nears
Fund children's education from equity gains
Approach retirement; focus on capital protection

aps.reasoning: All six equity-line items (Multi-Cap, two Large-&-Mid, two ELSS, one Aggressive-Hybrid) are actively managed funds; no index fund or ETF appears, so passive_instrument_fraction = 0. Rebalancing is only once-a-year with a 5%-band, giving a moderate turnover_score of 0.6. Expense ratios around 1.0-1.1% are accepted without comment and fund picks are justified by past out-performance, so cost_emphasis_score = 0. Each rationale cites category-average beating returns and fund-manager skill, showing preference for research/alpha over cost, hence research_vs_cost_score = 0.2. Strategy targets 20-year goals, step-up SIPs and pre-defined glide-path, so time_horizon_alignment_score = 0.8. Portfolio categories (Multi-Cap, Large-&-Mid, ELSS, Aggressive-Hybrid) are explicitly active mandates, absent any passive alternatives, so portfolio_activeness_score = 0.

pqs.reasoning:
goal_alignment: 80/100 — 20-year horizon for retirement & 15-year education goal are matched with high-equity growth funds, but the 5-year house goal is over-exposed to volatile equity; only 10 % in dynamic hybrid is inadequate short-term protection.
diversification: 60/100 — good spread across large/mid/small via multi-cap and L&M funds, two different AMCs chosen for ELSS, but all six holdings are pure Indian equity or equity-taxed hybrids; no international, debt, gold, or defensive alternatives; concentration in similar market-cap buckets.
risk_return_appropriateness: 70/100 — projected 11 % bear-case is better than typical aggressive portfolios, but 90 % equity-tagged exposure is still high for a self-declared moderate investor needing money in five years; no contingency/emergency bucket mentioned.
internal_consistency: 65/100 — rationale claims tax-efficient equity wrappers yet lumps the 5-year house need into the same high-risk basket; rebalancing guideline talks of moving to “under-weight areas” but no under-weight asset exists today; projected returns cite 15 % CAGR while rationale says “reliable 21 %” past returns — mixing past & future.
tax_efficiency: 90/100 — recognises 30 % slab, uses ELSS to harvest 80C and equity-taxed hybrid instead of slab-taxed debt, correctly skips non-equity debt funds; only nit-pick: very small AUM ELSS choices add manager risk, not tax risk.
